stemHtml: The tilt of an Airborne Weather Radar is lowered whilst searching for thunderstorms in high-level cruise flight. Why is that?

options.a: The greatest reflectivity occurs from wet hail and rain, these elements usually exist in the lower levels of a thunderstorm.
options.b: The greatest reflectivity occurs from dry hail and snow, these elements exist mainly in the lower levels of a thunderstorm.
options.c: Ground clutter is more evident and therefore less likely to be confused with thunderstorm activity.
options.d: Ground clutter enhances the display by automatically turning up the gain and increasing the likelihood of detecting thunderstorm activity.
